methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $87M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Osaro is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Derik Pridmore and Michael Kahane that has raised $86.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of nine private robotics firms and one public peer, with six same-stage private companies forming the primary peer set: 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised ($87M) as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $165M. The peer-median method references the six same-sector, same-stage comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $1.1B with a range of $300M to $10.0B, reflecting material dispersion driven largely by 1X Technologies' outlier position.

The two-method triangulation produces a valuation range of $140M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $620M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in early-stage robotics valuations, where capital intensity, path to revenue, and technology risk vary substantially across the peer set. Material sensitivities include the degree to which Osaro's technology and market position align with higher-valuation peers versus the median, as well as visibility into near-term revenue traction and customer concentration.
